entries.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
entries.description: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”

entries.title: Why Wall Street thinks US memory maker Micron is the next Nvidia
entries.description: Eager to find more public AI-related companies that may do as well as Nvidia, Wall Street investors think they've found a winner with Micron.

entries.title: SoftBank’s CEO isn’t the only one with questions about Elon Musk’s orbital data center hype
entries.description: Not everyone is buying Elon Musk’s vision for orbital data centers.

entries.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
entries.description: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

entries.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
entries.description: When confronted with cancer, Conno Christou fed everything tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

entries.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like  models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
entries.description: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

entries.title: Trump administration releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
entries.description: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

entries.title: Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
entries.description: Google is expanding Gemini’s personalized AI image generation to eligible free users in the U.S., allowing the chatbot to create images based on your interests and data from connected Google apps.

entries.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
entries.description: As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.

entries.title: South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘RAMageddon’
entries.description: The world's two largest memory chip companies vow to build more memory lab fabs as South Korea positions itself as an AI tech powerhouse country.

entries.title: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
entries.description: The startup, which runs a popular free AI leaderboard, launched its commercial service just last September.

entries.title: Cursor now has a mobile app for guiding your coding agent on the go
entries.description: Cursor has launched a new mobile app for remote oversight over coding agents.

entries.title: TIDAL cracks down on AI music by cutting off monetization
entries.description: In addition, TIDAL will use automated tools to remove AI-generated music that attempts to impersonate an artist or a group, the company said.

entries.title: Robot hand company settles Tesla trade secret suit and announces $11M raise
entries.description: The startup, Proception, is taking a unique approach to collecting training data to tackle one of the hardest problems in robotics: hands.

entries.title: Omen AI’s plan to optimize data centers is all wet
entries.description: Omen AI raised a $31 million Series A to monitor chip coolant and stop bacterial outbreaks in data centers.
